title: Good: Idempotent init/start/stop functions with clear pre/postconditions
description:
lora_l2.c implements idempotent lifecycle functions with explicit state checks:

1. lora_l2.c:243-246 - lichen_lora_l2_init() returns 0 immediately if already initialized, under mutex to prevent TOCTOU
2. lora_l2.c:296-299 - lichen_lora_l2_start() returns 0 if already running
3. lora_l2.c:345-348 - lichen_lora_l2_stop() returns 0 if not running
4. lora_l2.c:408-411 - lichen_lora_l2_deinit() rejects call if still running with actionable error message

5. lora_l2.c:287-293 - start() checks aborted flag BEFORE mutex, explaining that module requires re-init after forced abort

What goes right: Callers can safely call init/start multiple times without tracking state themselves. Error messages tell the caller what to do ('call stop() first'). The aborted state is handled as a distinct failure mode requiring explicit recovery.

Worth defending: This idempotent design makes the API resilient to double-init bugs from multiple callers (main.c vs NET_DEVICE_INIT). The explicit error states prevent silent failures.
close_reason: Positive finding - pattern to preserve, not an issue
